source: Top up players before each Arcane Expulsion
reason: AoE knockback hits everyone; pre-heal + position to wall are shared jobs. Icy Veins lists under All Roles.

source: Dispel as many players as you can from Ethereal Shackles
reason: Dispel archetype is all; healer is primary cleanser but immunity/self-dispel and defensive fallback keep it shared. Method notes it targets the tank but any dispeller/freedom removes it.

source: Tanks be ready with a defensive for the Repulsing Slam arcane hit and knockback
reason: Both guides place under Tank; only the tank is hit by the arcane buster + knockback ('Tanks be ready with a defensive for the Repulsing Slam').

source: During Refueling Protocol phase, use it to your advantage... 20% increased damage
reason: Soak + burn-window combo; everyone soaks Energy Orbs and dumps damage simultaneously, so the soak job keeps it shared rather than a DPS-only burn.

source: Soak every Orb so none reach the boss
reason: Classic all-role soak; everyone loosely spreads to soak every orb so none reach the boss (Method).

source: Watch stacks; defensive if you soak several
reason: Per-soaker stacking DoT each player manages with personal defensives; the Do addresses the individual, not the healer. Evidence overrides the raid-damage 'healer' prior.

source: Step in to clear Runic Mark / survive Wave of Silence; time entry so you don't need to cast while inside
reason: Every player uses the zone to clear Runic Mark and survive Wave of Silence; both guides address all roles uniformly.

source: Use your defensives during Runic Mark and get into the Suppression Zone
reason: Spread-out targeting random players who spread then step into zone; Icy Veins All Roles. Healer spot-heal is supportive, not the defining reaction.

source: Before Seranel Sunlash finishes its Wave of Silence cast, be sure to be under the Suppression Zone
reason: Every player must be in the Suppression Zone before cast finishes or eat lethal damage + silence; both guides under All Roles.

source: If you have a purge then use it to remove the Hastening Ward buff, otherwise tanks will need to use a defensive
reason: Purge-soothe primary (any enemy-dispel removes the buff); if no purge the tank must major-defensive the empowered hits, a genuine secondary.

source: Cleave the clones — shared HP. Below 50% every player incl. tank is targeted by a clone
reason: Shared-HP clones everyone cleaves; below 50% every player incl. tank is targeted. Method: cleave all clones since they share health.

source: Be mindful when Cosmic Sting happens as the follow-up effect of Void Secretions will hurt if people are slow to reposition
reason: Targeted player (any role) moves to drop the puddle at the edge; Icy Veins Healer note is monitoring/spot-heal support, not the primary actor.

source: If you get Neural Link debuff, make sure to hug the right copy of the boss
reason: Random players run to their linked clone to clear debuff + strip shield; anyone can be hit and performs the move (Method).

source: Run away from the pull-in if targeted by Astral Grasp, plus avoid the circles around all the clones
reason: Pull-in plus Cosmic Radiation aura affects all; everyone uses movement to escape. Both guides address all roles.

source: Make sure to never touch the Void Torrent beams outlining the pizza-slices
reason: Everyone avoids the beams and holds arena position; classic ground-void-zone, no role singled out.

source: Spread your team-mates through the entire room... so you can bounce the Unstable Energy ball
reason: Every player covers a pizza slice to catch the bouncing ball; one missed soak stacks Void Destruction and wipes. Both guides All Roles.

source: Tanks avoid cleaving allies with the Hulking Fragment hit, then back away from the boss and get dispelled by your healer to remove the Magic DoT
reason: Tank-buster: tank mitigates, faces away, drops ichor away from DPS; healer must dispel the Umbral Splinters Magic DoT right after — a genuine secondary (Method).

source: Use your major healing cooldowns during Devouring Entropy debuff
reason: Icy Veins puts major healing CDs under Healer as the defining reaction, but the Do/Method also have individuals defensive on long timers — healer-led but partly shared, hence low.

source: Keep healing through it; no melee to dodge
reason: Unavoidable ranged raid damage with no dodge/positional job; Do says keep healing through it — healer sustains.

source: Trigger it (tank) so it doesn't go off untouched
reason: Do explicitly assigns the tank to trigger it so it doesn't go off untouched; no other role named as reactor, overriding the soak 'all' prior.

source: If you get targeted by Carrion Swoop, make sure to enter a Freezing Trap to avoid any damage. Be mindful that anyone else caught in the Nekraxx path will get a ton of damage
reason: Fixated player enters a trap while everyone else clears the Nekraxx path; both jobs are real and split across the group.

source: Avoid the Freezing Traps unless you are targeted by Carrion Swoop
reason: Whole group avoids the traps (or the Carrion Swoop target uses one); positioning/dodge is shared.

source: Stand still if targeted by Barrage to allow other players to avoid the frontal channel and stacking Magic slow it applies
reason: Targeted player stands still while everyone else dodges the cone; both responses span the group.

source: Dodge the Fetid Quillstorm
reason: Universal ground-void-zone dodge; both guides list it under All Roles with no role exception.

source: Use your major healing cooldowns during Infected Pinions cast
reason: Both guides put 'use your defensive' under All Roles and the dispel is any dispeller; healer majors are support, so this is raid damage the whole group survives through with healer secondary.

source: Tanks use a defensive or Bleed Cleanse on Flanking Spear
reason: Tank-targeted bleed requiring active mitigation / bleed cleanse; no other role has a distinct job.

source: Make sure to split damage between the bosses, as Muro'jin will cast Revive Pet when Nekraxx dies
reason: Balance-kill: whole group routes even damage so Nekraxx never dies first to trigger it.

source: Nekraxx will gain stacks of Bestial Wrath when Muro'jin dies
reason: Mirror balance-kill: everyone keeps both bosses even so Muro'jin never dies first to empower Nekraxx.

source: Kite the mobs created by Wrest Phantoms such that they collide with each other to explode, but make sure to stagger these collisions so that your healer has time to top your group
reason: Whole group kites, CCs, and collides phantoms while staggering for the healer and dodging Veiled Presence pools.

source: Save damage for the Necrotic Convergence channel so you can quickly destroy the shield and interrupt the cast
reason: Burn-window: the defining job is dumping burst to break Deathshroud fast, then interrupt; the damage race is DPS-led.

source: Tanks use a defensive for the Drain Soul channel
reason: Both guides: tank presses a major defensive each Drain Soul channel; tank-buster with no other role's job.

source: all players should be ready to avoid the Unmake frontal sweep
reason: Standard frontal-cone dodge; both guides place it under All Roles.

source: During the Crush Souls cast, the boss will target 3 players, make sure to group closer to allow for a better Soulbind Totem cleave
reason: The 3 targeted players group their totem drops, then the whole group focuses the totems; positioning + add-kill are shared.

source: focus these totems down as they channel Soulbind into random players, pulling them in
reason: Kill-priority add anyone with damage burns before it pulls players in; no single role owns it.

source: Tanks use a defensive and move away from your group during the Spiritbreaker combo attack as it drops a large puddle underneath you
reason: Tank-targeted combo: major defensive, move away, and manage the knockback/puddle; consequences only the tank handles.

source: Use your major healing cooldowns during Deathgorged Vessel cast
reason: Everyone dodges the circles (the universal job), with healer majors covering the passive ramp as support; shared response defaults to all.

source: make sure to use an interrupt or CC the 6 Malignant Souls to gain the stacking Spectral Residue buff
reason: Whole group is sent to the bridge to race the debuff, avoid Lost Souls, and interrupt/CC the 6 Malignant Souls; identical jobs for everyone.

source: Avoid the Lost Souls as they will root you with Cries of the Fallen
reason: Proximity-bait root anyone on the gauntlet avoids; universal avoidance with no role distinction.

source: Never pass through an active Leyline Array as it will become lethal without defensives.
reason: Icy Veins lists this under All Roles; every player must navigate the room without crossing active beams. Shared job, matches ground-void-zone prior of all.

source: If you get targeted by the Reflux Charge, make sure to destroy as many Leyline Array as possible.
reason: The debuff lands on any role and that player walks through intersections; no role-specific assignment in either guide. Shared mechanic, matches all prior.

source: healers be prepared with cooldowns to remove the healing absorb it applies
reason: Everyone outranges impact, baits to edges, and minds knockback (all); Icy Veins Healer section calls out the party-wide Sparkburn heal-absorb as a distinct healer cooldown job.

source: Sidestep the Flux Collapse ground puddles.
reason: Icy Veins lists sidestepping Flux Collapse puddles under All Roles; every player baits the flow and avoids Arcane Spill. Matches all prior.

source: Tanks be aware that Chief Corewright Kasreth does not use melee swings, and instead hits you for arcane damage with the instant cast ability called Arcane Zap.
reason: Method/Icy Veins describe Arcane Zap as the boss's tank-targeting arcane melee substitute; the tank is the sole recipient. Note: the 'interrupt' on the Do cell belongs to the trash Corewright Arcanist's channel, not the boss tank hit, so tank-buster holds. Medium: interrupt phrasing muddies it.

source: Be mindful of your surroundings when Eclipsing Step is on you.
reason: Targets two players of any role; Icy Veins under All Roles, Method says avoid cleaving allies and defensive. Shared spread/defensive job, matches all prior.

source: keep interrupts on the Nullify cast and tanks point the Dreadflail away from your group when it gains the Void Lash buff
reason: Killing the spawned adds and interrupting Nullify is a whole-group job (all); Method explicitly assigns the tank to point the Dreadflail away during Void Lash as a distinct secondary.

source: Use your major defensive cooldowns during Umbral Lash, be mindful as it applies a follow-up Void Gash debuff.
reason: Both guides place Umbral Lash in the Tank section requiring major tank defensives plus the Void Gash follow-up; classic tank-buster on the threat-holder.

source: save all of your offensive abilities to do maximum damage as the boss takes 300% more damage
reason: Burn window: dodge the flare then stand in the frontal for the 300% amp, primary job is dumping DPS cooldowns; Icy Veins/Method Healer note the continuous ticking damage as a distinct healer-cooldown job. Matches burn-window dps prior.

source: Make sure any adds are dead before this channel ends, otherwise the boss will consume them with Devour The Unworthy.
reason: Whole group must kill adds before the channel ends or the boss empowers; a collective kill-priority deadline. Matches kill-priority-add all prior.

source: Use your major defensive cooldowns when Lothraxion is casting Searing Rend on you.
reason: Both guides place Searing Rend in the Tank section: tank defensive plus drop the lingering puddles out of the way. Tank-buster on threat-holder.

source: Use your major healing cooldowns during each Brilliant Dispersion.
reason: Three targeted players of any role spread before detonation and use personals, then everyone stays >5yds from Fractured Images (all); Icy Veins Healer section assigns a major healing cooldown each cast for the hard DoT.

source: Flicker, which hits anyone in the path of the dash so make sure to avoid it.
reason: Method: hits anyone in the dash path, so everyone avoids it; no role-specific action. Matches charge all prior.

source: find the one without horns of light and interrupt it to return to the boss phase
reason: Icy Veins lists under All Roles; every player must ID the hornless copy and someone kicks it while never kicking an image (wrong kick = Core Exposure). Collective identify + interrupt, matches all prior.

source: Use your defensives if you get Flaming Updraft debuff and place it near the corner
reason: Debuffed players of any role move and drop the puddle to a corner; healer spot-heals as a distinct secondary job.

source: healer pops major cooldowns for the heavy party damage through the channel
reason: Knockback bracing and twister dodging fall on everyone; healer has a distinct cooldown job for the heavy channel damage.

source: All Roles: Be mindful of the Fire Breath frontal during Burning Gale channel
reason: Everyone dodges the four rotating frontal cones; standard frontal-cone group response.

source: Tank: Use your major defensives during Searing Beak
reason: Heavy hit plus Fire DoT lands only on the tank; tank defensive covering both is the sole reaction.

source: Both bosses must die simultaneously in order to avoid dealing with their Broken Bond Enrage effect
reason: Balance-kill: every DPS routes target damage and tank keeps both together; no single role owns it.

source: Interrupt Kalis's Shadow Bolt casts
reason: Interruptible cast on a random target; anyone with a kick rotates in.

source: make sure the player targeted by Latch's Heaving Yank runs behind her to stop the group damage
reason: Whoever is hit by Heaving Yank (any role) runs behind Kalis; positional gimmick owned by the tagged player.

source: use Curse dispels to remove the Curse Of Darkness debuff from both targets. If you can't remove these debuffs then be ready with CCs to keep the Dark Entity away
reason: Curse dispel is a shared dispeller job; absent a dispel everyone CCs/kites the Dark Entity. No single role exclusive.

source: The only way to stop Kalis's Debilitating Shriek is to use Latch's Heaving Yank and 'hook' it back
reason: The yanked player (any role) reacts by running behind Kalis to cancel the shriek; positional gimmick on whoever is tagged.

source: All Roles: During the Splattering Spew, use your personal defensive and place the Gunk Splatter in an appropriate place; Healer: Use your healing cooldowns during each Splattering Spew
reason: All roles spread and place puddles tidily; healer has a distinct healing-cooldown job for the group damage.

source: Tank: Use your active mitigation before each Bone Hack from Latch
reason: Channel directed at the tank requiring active mitigation; no other role action called out.

source: All Roles: You can bait Reckless Leap as the furthest player away, just make sure to use your defensives. All party members just dodge the following Falling Rubble ground puddles.
reason: Baiting as furthest player rotates between DPS and tank; everyone dodges the rubble. Both jobs group-wide.

source: Tank: Use your major defensive cooldowns during Rampage cast
reason: Sustained channel on the current tank target; tank major defensive is the sole reactive job.

source: Stand near an ally when Intimidating Shout is cast, you don't need to stack your entire party so feel free to have both a ranged group and melee group
reason: Fear-if-alone; everyone must be near an ally. Classic stack-up group response.

source: Tanks collect these adds and cleave them down while keeping interrupts on the Chain Lightning cast; Healer: Top the whole party before each Rallying Bellow cast
reason: Three jobs land at once (healer tops, tank collects adds, everyone interrupts/cleaves); no role dominates.

source: the player fixated by the boss's Bladestorm needs to kite to avoid the damage
reason: Fixate-chase: whoever is fixated (any role) kites; tank cannot taunt it off.

source: Kill the adds to drop it; don't tunnel the boss
reason: Flavor aura; everyone simply kills adds to drop it, no role-specific reaction.

source: All Roles: Dodge the Arrow Rain
reason: Everyone dodges the AoE circles; standard ground-void-zone group response.

source: All Roles: Clear your Squall Leap debuff by taking the Turbulent Arrows which knock you upwards
reason: Group-wide stacking debuff; every player touches a Turbulent Arrow to clear their own stacks.

source: Use the Turbulent Arrows to avoid being hit by the Billowing Wind wave
reason: Shared positional tool every player uses to cleanse and body-block knockbacks.

source: pre-positioning and using a ping to tell your party members which arrow you plan to use so that no one doubles up
reason: Every player pre-positions on an arrow to dodge the wave; positional gimmick on the whole group.

source: stand still if you are the target and use a defensive or combat drop to stop the channel; Healer: Spot-heal the party member that gets Bolt Gale
reason: Do text names the targeted player (any role) standing still and using a defensive/combat-drop; healer spot-heals as a distinct secondary. Evidence overrides the healer prior.

source: Tank: Use your active mitigation during each Tempest Slash; for tanks there's the Tempest Slash hit which also has a large knockback attached to it
reason: Heavy hit plus knockback aimed at the tank; tank mitigates and positions back-to-arrow. Exclusively the tank's job.

source: If targeted by Gust Shot, spread out to avoid cleaving your allies and do your best to overlap any existing puddles as this will clear them from the arena
reason: Targeted player (any role) spreads and overlaps puddles to clear them; spread-out response on all.

source: Stack together and move in one direction when Germinate cast is going through
reason: Icy Veins All Roles: whole group stacks and moves one direction to cluster spawns for cleave; no single role owns it.

source: Healer must watch for Burst Forth as it does heavy damage to the entire group; tanks collect threat on every awakened Hungry Lasher
reason: Heavy unavoidable party-wide hit the healer pre-tops/CDs (prior=healer); both guides give the tank a distinct job picking up every awakened Lasher.

source: tanks make sure you pick them up and have players rotate poison dispels on them
reason: Poison dispels rotate across any dispeller (all); tank has a separate explicit threat-grab/kite job, so tank secondary.

source: Use active mitigation once you see the boss casting Barkbreaker on you
reason: Both guides Tank section: direct tank-buster with physical-damage-amp; tank presses active mitigation.

source: Sidestep the Branch Out cast; Swap to it, interrupt Healing Touch; tank taunts on spawn
reason: Add-swap + interrupt is an All Roles job; tank has the explicit separate task of taunting the add on spawn.

source: Interrupt the Ancient Branch's Healing Touch at all cost
reason: Interruptible-cast: any kick stops it; Icy Veins All Roles. Matches archetype RoleTag all.

source: Stand in the Abundance circle (8 yd) to cleanse Splinterbark; time the Branch death
reason: Every player steps into the 8yd Abundance circle to cleanse their own bleed; shared soak/positioning job.

source: Tanks make sure to have a defensive up for every Savage Peck hit and DoT
reason: Both guides Tank section: defensive up for every Savage Peck hit and DoT; direct tank-buster.

source: All players should be ready to avoid the random target Overpowering Gust frontal
reason: Random-target frontal everyone must sidestep; Icy Veins All Roles.

source: Be ready to top your teammates when Crawth is casting Deafening Screech; use group defensives or healing cooldowns
reason: Prior=all, but both guides put the reaction on the healer's group defensive/healing CD for the unavoidable DoT spike; loose spread is incidental. Evidence names the healer as primary reactor.

source: Grab 3 balls, throw into a goal to cancel the cast
reason: Group positional task: everyone grabs and throws balls to cancel the cast; no single role owns it.

spell: Goal of the Rushing Winds → Gale Force
source: dodge the patrolling cyclones; spawning patrolling tornadoes around the platform and winds that push you back
reason: Triggers patrolling cyclones + pushback winds everyone must dodge for the rest of the fight; universal avoidance.

spell: Goal of the Searing Blaze → Firestorm
source: pop Bloodlust/CDs into the damage amp; If possible save damage cooldown or bloodlust for when you score on this goal
reason: 12s +75% damage window; both guides say dump CDs/Bloodlust here. Burn-window job is DPS-led (prior=dps).

source: Spread out and soak orbs before they reach Vexamus; the rest of the team can help soaking the Arcane Orbs
reason: Both guides direct the whole group to spread and soak before orbs reach the boss; tank 'more appropriate' but team helps, so primary is a group soak.

source: Aim the Arcane Expulsion frontal away from your team; point this away from your group and use a defensive
reason: Prior=all, but both guides put it in the Tank section: frontal aimed at the tank who must face it away and defensive. Tank owns the reaction.

source: Defensive; drop pools toward the room edge out of the way; targets 3 random players, use a defensive if needed
reason: Hits 3 random players of any role; everyone spreads and uses a personal defensive. Shared spread-out response.

source: Use your healing cooldowns once the boss casts Arcane Fissure; Healing CD for the hit
reason: Prior=all (knockback/void). Icy Veins Healer section explicitly calls healing CDs for the unavoidable hit; everyone also brace/sidestep, but the healer leads the response.

source: Be topped before pull; everyone is topped before engaging and pull the boss towards the entrance
reason: Prior=healer (raid-damage), but the named reaction is everyone pre-topped AND tank positioning the boss/rifts; shared job, so evidence yields all over the prior.

source: Keep a couple stacks for Haste but don't greed to 3 in a bad spot; place the rift well — then dodge the orbs
reason: Each player manages their own stacks and places their own rift safely; universal positional responsibility.

source: Use a personal defensive when you get an Energy Bomb cast on you; loosely spread to avoid cleaving allies
reason: Random target uses personal defensive and everyone spreads to avoid splash; Icy Veins All Roles.

source: healer spot-heals; The boss will occasionally cast Arcane Missiles on a random target, so top them up quickly!
reason: Both guides Healer section: spot-heal/top the random target quickly (prior=healer); targeted player only personals if overlapping Energy Bomb.

source: Run out / use movement; tanks make sure she's positioned so players don't get pulled onto a rift
reason: Pulls all players; everyone uses movement to escape. Tank positioning is secondary support, not ownership, so all primary.

source: Tanks should also watch out for the Astral Blast casts, using a defensive if needed
reason: Both guides Tank section: heavy arcane tank hit requiring active mitigation; tank-buster.

source: everyone should be ready to avoid the random target Null Palm frontal
reason: Random-target frontal cone everyone must dodge; Method 'everyone should be ready to avoid' — no single-role owner.

source: If Zuraal the Ascended targets you with Decimate, make sure to be away from the middle for better space management
reason: Any player can be targeted and must drop the puddle at the edge; universal positioning job, prior 'all' holds.

source: Tank pops a major defensive
reason: Icy Veins Tank section: 'Use your major defensive cooldowns during the Void Slash tank buster' — textbook tank-buster.

source: Healers CD the DoT; slow/stun/knockback or long-CC the oozes and burn one at a time
reason: Icy Veins Healer names the group DoT as the healer-CD job (life-threatening, primary); DPS/all swap-CC the Coalesced Void is a genuine second job, so healer over prior 'all'.

source: don't get knocked into a Void Sludge pool; oozes must be dead/CC'd first; Make sure players are topped
reason: Method: get topped, CC oozes, don't get knocked into a puddle — the reaction (position/survive the knockback) is shared by everyone, not the healer alone, so prior 'healer' loses to evidence.

source: Don't touch them; clear them via Phase Dash circle
reason: Ground avoidance: don't touch the bombs, clear via Phase Dash — everyone's job, prior 'all' holds.

source: Use the dash to detonate/clear bombs safely
reason: Positional gimmick the whole group uses to cleave bombs; no single-role owner.

source: Clear all bombs first; leftover bombs get tank/immunity-soaked before cast; healers top group
reason: Method: all clear bombs first (primary); leftover bombs tank/immunity-soaked; healers top before the cast — two genuine secondaries, primary stays 'all'.

source: Tank uses active mitigation
reason: Icy Veins Tank: 'Use your active mitigation during Rending Void cast' — tank-buster by definition.

source: Assign 2+ interrupts, ideally a ranged kick on every even cast
reason: Assign 2+ interrupts / ranged kick every even cast — canonical all-role interrupt rotation.

source: Spot-heal / bleed-cleanse / defensive the target
reason: Icy Veins Healer: 'Spot-heal the person that gets Shadow Pounce Bleed' — primary reaction is the healer's spot-heal/cleanse; Method's broader 'cleanses or defensives' keeps confidence medium.

source: Keep an interrupt rotation on it to cut the damage
reason: Icy Veins All Roles: 'Interrupt Mind Blast' — group kick rotation (primary all); it targets the tank, so tank mitigating the leak is a real secondary.

source: Dodge the waves; defensive if hit
reason: Dodge the Umbral Waves — directional avoidance everyone shares, prior 'all' holds.

source: Targeted players defensive; healers CD the group hit
reason: Method: targeted players use a defensive (shared when targeted, primary); Icy Veins Healer preps CDs for the group-wide hit — genuine healer secondary.

source: Cleave them down fast (tank positions boss near them); defensive
reason: Icy Veins All Roles: focus the tentacles before Collapsing Void — kill-priority cleave for everyone; tank's positioning note is not a unique reactive job.

source: Move under the boss (safe spot); tentacles must be dead first; group defensives + healing CDs
reason: Method: dash under the boss to the safe spot, group defensives + healing CDs — positional/knockback survival everyone reacts to identically.

source: Healers spread CDs across the sustained damage
reason: Method: 'healers will need to spread their cooldowns out to handle this consistent damage' — defining job is healer CD management; raid-damage RoleTag healer confirmed.

source: Stay topped; aim/face it through active Notes to silence all 6 → triggers intermission
reason: Targeted players aim the beam through Notes to silence them — core positional/aim mechanic everyone participates in.

source: Avoid the Note circles; rush to silence all Notes before Anguish snowballs unhealable
reason: Avoid the Note circles and race to silence before Anguish becomes unhealable — universal avoidance + speed-of-execution job.

source: Move with the rotation to avoid
reason: Icy Veins All Roles: 'Dodge the Disintegrate' — rotating beams everyone keeps moving to avoid.

source: Tank watches stacks, defensive at 3
reason: Method/Icy Veins Tank: watch stacks, defensive at 3 before the burst — stacking tank-buster.

source: Save/pool DPS cooldowns for this window
reason: Method: 'save your damage cooldowns for that' — taxonomy's canonical burn-window example, RoleTag dps.

source: Be mindful with your position before each Gale Surge cast as it knocks you backwards and spawns Coalesced Wind wind orb
reason: Knockback every player must pre-position for and drop the orb safely; no single role owns it. Prior all, evidence all.

source: Use your major healing cooldowns during Fan of Blades
reason: Group-wide bleed; both guides assign major healing cooldowns to the healer as the primary reaction. Defensives/cleanse are secondary flavor.

source: Avoid being hit by Wind Chakram, it 1-shots; Move the boss away from Wind Chakram to prevent the melee players from dying from the returning chakram
reason: Everyone must dodge the one-shot projectile both passes; tank has a distinct second job repositioning the boss so the return misses melee.

source: Dodge the incoming Gale Surge during Chakram Vortex
reason: Moving tornadoes every player must dodge; no role-specific reaction.

source: Non-tanks should be ready to intercept the Energize beams Lesser Constructs shoot towards the boss in sets of 3, using defensives as needed
reason: Soak job is shared by all non-tanks (DPS and healer both intercept beams); a group soak assignment, so all rather than a single role. Confidence medium since the prior for soak is all but the work explicitly excludes the tank.

source: Everyone avoid it; step into the AoE under the boss if needed
reason: Frontal every player must dodge; Do text and Method both say all players avoid it.

source: Make sure people are topped-up before each Supernova
reason: Full-energy raid nuke; healer pre-tops and calls group defensives as the primary reaction. Unavoidable raid-damage, healer-led.

source: Tank points it away from beam-soakers and steps out of it
reason: Both guides put this solely on the tank: point the frontal/line away from beam-soakers and step out. Evidence overrides the frontal-cone all prior.

source: Dodge any incoming Defensive Protocol heat waves
reason: 5-yard AoE under the boss all players must avoid; no role distinction.

source: Make sure to be in melee at all times to avoid Araknath from casting Blast Wave
reason: Suppressed entirely by the tank staying in melee on the stationary boss; only tank positioning prevents the cast. Evidence overrides positional-gimmick all prior.

source: Stack closer to the boss during Sunbreak to spawn Sunwing in a position where you can cleave it simultaneously
reason: Whole group stacks on the boss to spawn the add in a cleave spot and burns it; everyone participates.

source: Use your crowd-control spells when the Burning Pursuit happens; Spot-heal any player that gets Burning Pursuit from the Sunwing
reason: Everyone CCs/kills the fixating Sunwing while the target kites (all); healer has a distinct second job spot-healing the chased player.

source: always be mindful of their post-death effect Smoldering Egg (if another Sunwing spawns on top of the Smoldering Egg it will respawn it)
reason: Whole group must avoid killing Sunwings on top of eggs; shared awareness, DPS especially since they do the killing.

source: Line of sight during Searing Quills
reason: Everyone including the tank breaks LoS behind the central pillar simultaneously; positional gimmick applies to all.

source: Use your active mitigation for Burning Claws
reason: Heavy fire tank hit; tank presses active mitigation every cast. Classic tank-buster, prior tank.

source: Make sure to always be in melee range of the boss to prevent it from casting Screech
reason: Cast only when no one is in melee on the stationary boss; suppressing it is the tank's positional job. Evidence overrides positional-gimmick all prior.

source: everyone swaps to the mob ASAP and uses stuns to slow it down; Trying to get High Sage Viryx closer to the person that gets Cast Down so you can cleave both targets
reason: Whole group swaps and stuns the add immediately (all); tank has a distinct job dragging the boss closer to cleave both targets.

source: If targeted by a Lens Flare make sure to get away from your teammates and place the Blazing Ground puddles the way that will allow for a better place management
reason: Any player can be targeted and must run to the sides placing the fire trail safely; everyone else avoids the trail. Movement for all.

source: healers watch these players, and if targeted multiple times in a row be ready to use a defensive
reason: DoT on multiple players; healer watches and tops the targets as the primary reaction. Defensives by the hit players are secondary.

source: keep an interrupt rotation on Solar Blast to reduce your tank's damage intake
reason: Everyone shares the interrupt rotation (all); uninterrupted casts land on the tank as a tank-buster, giving the tank a distinct secondary stake.

source: make sure to place the Ore Chunks closer to each other
reason: Icy Veins All Roles; two random players each place a chunk and avoid overlap — shared spread/place job, no role owns it.

source: Orebreaker targets the tank, use a defensive and make sure to overlap one of the Ore Chunks to destroy it
reason: Both guides Tank section; targets the tank, only the tank overlaps a chunk and pre-defensives the stun.

source: During Glacial Overload, make sure to stay behind an Ore Chunks
reason: Icy Veins All Roles; every player must hide behind an Ore Chunk for cover — universal positional job.

source: Use defensives during Cryostomp, as it will do group-wide damage and give 2 people a nasty Cryoshards debuff
reason: Group-wide hit everyone defensives through (All Roles section) plus a distinct healer job — dispel the Cryoshards target ASAP — so all over the healer prior, healer secondary.

source: make sure to put the best healing talents possible here!
reason: Sustained passive aura placed in the Healer section ('best healing talents possible') — a throughput ramp the healer owns, overriding the pulsing-aura all prior.

source: both bosses share health due to Necrolink, make sure to cleave them
reason: Both guides: keep both bosses together and cleave — universal positioning/damage-routing job.

source: Interrupt Krick's Death Bolt
reason: Icy Veins All Roles names it the priority interrupt; anyone with a kick rotates on it.

source: focus all the Shades of Krick first and do your best to interrupt Shadowbind cast
reason: All Roles; everyone swaps to cleave the Shades — kill-priority add with a universal swap.

source: Interrupt, or decurse / freedom / drop-combat to break it
reason: Multiple responses across roles (interrupt, decurse, freedom, vanish); Icy Veins All Roles — no single role owns it.

source: Use your major defensive cooldowns during Blight Smash cast and stay away from the Blight ground ichors
reason: Both guides Tank section; tank-targeted buster, tank major-defensives and drops the puddle out of the way.

source: Sidestep the Plague Globs cast and stay away from the ground ichor
reason: Puddles spawn under ~4 random players; Icy Veins All Roles — everyone pre-spreads and sidesteps.

source: Kite the boss if targeted, otherwise try and save damage CDs to maximize boss cleave
reason: Fixate hits a random player who kites; others reposition/save CDs — universal, tank can't taunt it off.

source: Spot-heal people during Shadow Lance cast
reason: Icy Veins Healer section: 'Spot-heal people during Shadow Lance' — a single-target spike the healer triages.

source: If you get Rime Blast debuff, make sure to place it on top of the Infused Bone Piles & Bone Piles
reason: All Roles; any tagged player walks the debuff onto a Bone Pile — positional job for whoever is hit.

source: Use your major healing cooldowns during Bone Infusion cast
reason: Icy Veins Healer section calls for major healing CDs on the cast; a group-wide spike the healer leads, matching the raid-damage healer prior.

source: During Army of the Dead, focus the Scourge Plaguespreader and interrupt their cast at all costs
reason: Everyone swaps to kill-priority adds (Plaguespreaders) plus a distinct healer job — dispel Rotting Strikes disease stacking on the tank — so all primary, healer secondary.

source: Swap to this mob ASAP and keep an interrupt rotation on its cast
reason: Method and Do agree: everyone swaps ASAP and rotates interrupts on the cast — universal kick/swap job.

source: Use your major defensive cooldowns during Scourgelord's Brand and avoid being hit by Scourgelord's Reckoning
reason: Both guides Tank section; targets the tank with a knockback + follow-up leap — tank defensives and repositions to dodge the second hit.

source: Sidestep Death's Grasp ground puddles
reason: All Roles; ground puddles every player sidesteps — classic ground-void-zone universal dodge.

source: Avoid being hit by the Ice Barrage during Army of the Dead phase
reason: All Roles; ground damage zones during the add phase everyone avoids.

source: Healer awareness
reason: Neither guide names this ability; Do text says only 'Healer awareness'. Raid-damage spike archetype defaults the response to the healer triaging, but guide silence keeps confidence low.
